topic: Gap analysis of dome author reactive changes 2026-04-17 (chg-20260417-1620-001..005) triggered by CNOTE-REACTIVE-001
change_summary: CNOTE-REACTIVE-001 asked the curmudgeon to review five 2026-04-17 poller change files (methodology.json, review-response.html, daily-ai-review.yml, claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes, ai_manifest/llms.txt/audit.html rewrites), cross-check existing pending work (expansions, human notes, open issues), and identify any dome-author concessions or changes that are NOT covered by existing work items.
our_argument_summary: This is a meta-audit, not a WIN or section review. The five 2026-04-17 dome-author commits collectively represent a documentary/architectural response to the pattern of critiques our review has pursued. We checked whether each commit's substance is covered by: (a) existing curmudgeon reviews; (b) integrated EXPs (170, 171, 172, 173, 174, 175, 176, 177); (c) open issues (ISS-1073 [fixed], ISS-1074, ISS-1087, ISS-1088, ISS-1089, ISS-1103, ISS-1105). Four of five changes are adequately covered. One change (chg-004: claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes disambiguating WIN-058/WIN-058b/WIN-062) is NOT engaged by our current WIN prose and lacks an open issue or pending EXP. One partial gap (registry_semantics self-caveat in ai_manifest.json) overlaps ISS-1087 but is not explicitly cited. The review-response.html analysis via EXP-171 correctly concluded all five strawman labels miss our actual positions.

EXP-173 Section 8.17 mentions claim_index.json only as part of the AI-directive read-order audit
change_coverage_matrix.residual_gaps: Our wins.json entries for 058, 058b, and 062 contain zero references to registry_integrity_notes, claim_key, or the slugs win-058-bedrock-kappa / win-058-theta-rule / win-062-tesla-wave-speed. This is the only major gap identified in this audit. Recommendation: file a new issue (suggested ISS-REACTIVE-WIN-COLLISION) asking the analyst to add a one-paragraph note to WIN-058, WIN-058b, and WIN-062 detail_evidence that (i) credits the dome author for acknowledging the collisions in the machine-readable API, (ii) distinguishes visible-prose collision (still unfixed: identical WIN numbers still render in wins.html) from machine-readable collision (now disambiguated via claim_key), (iii) notes this is another acknowledge-without-fix move consistent with the methodology.json pattern (ISS-1087) and belongs in our running catalog of such moves.

change_coverage_matrix.subject: Major doc rewrites across ai_manifest.json, llms.txt, llms-full.txt, audit.html, context.html (archival), killshot.html, index.html, model.html, predictions.html, sitemap.xml + new registry_semantics and agent_rules blocks + recommended_read_order ai_manifest->methodology->review-response->claim_index->wins.html

EXP-172 Section 2b.5 Live Dashboard Audit (integrated — quotes the dashboard stats)
EXP-176 Part 4.1 rewrite for 20->28 domain expansion (integrated)
change_coverage_matrix.residual_gaps: Minor: registry_semantics block in ai_manifest.json is a parallel self-caveat to methodology.json registry_counts (the latter tracked as ISS-1087). Not strictly necessary to cite both, but if ISS-1087 is ever closed by citing the methodology.json self-caveat, our prose should use language broad enough to cover both source files. Flag, not gap.

holes_found.description: Gap: WIN-058, WIN-058b, and WIN-062 detail_evidence prose contains zero references to registry_integrity_notes, claim_key, or the author-side disambiguation slugs (win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-058-theta-rule, win-062-tesla-wave-speed) introduced in claim_index.json on 2026-04-17 (chg-20260417-1620-004). Our review still flags visible-prose collisions as if unacknowledged. A dome defender can now reply that the API disambiguates these entries and our review is attacking a display-layer artifact the canonical data already resolves. That reply has partial weight: the machine-readable disambiguation IS a documentary concession that deserves to be credited. The full critique is the acknowledge-without-fix pattern (visible HTML still shows duplicate WIN-058 and duplicate WIN-062 rows in wins.html; the API-side claim_key is a workaround, not a fix) — but our prose does not yet make that distinction. This is the cleanest uncovered gap in the 2026-04-17 change set.
holes_found.recommendation: File a new decider issue (proposed id ISS-REACTIVE-WIN-COLLISION or similar): ask the analyst to add a short paragraph (or detail_extra block) to WIN-058, WIN-058b, and WIN-062 that (a) credits the registry_integrity_notes disambiguation as a documentary acknowledgement dated 2026-04-17 commit touching claim_index.json, (b) quotes the two registry_integrity_notes entries verbatim, (c) observes the visible-prose collisions remain (inspect wins.html for duplicate WIN-058 and duplicate WIN-062 rows), (d) catalogues this as another acknowledge-without-fix move alongside methodology.json registry_counts. This becomes a cross-reference hook the Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update block can link to when ISS-1088 gets closed by the documentary framing shift.

holes_found.description: Gap (flag): ai_manifest.json registry_semantics self-caveat is a parallel structure to methodology.json registry_counts (already tracked in ISS-1087). Our Part 2b.5 Live Dashboard Audit and Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update block could cite both source files together; currently they cite only methodology.json. Not a defect — ISS-1087 fix will close the higher-order point — but when the ISS-1087 patch is written, the wording should generalize to 'multiple author-side self-caveats in methodology.json and ai_manifest.json' rather than naming only one file.
holes_found.recommendation: When the analyst or decider resolves ISS-1087, use language that covers both methodology.json registry_counts AND ai_manifest.json registry_semantics. No new issue required — just a note on ISS-1087.

holes_found.description: Meta-observation: No single document in our review currently catalogs the April 2026 reactive-change set as a whole. The coverage is distributed across EXP-170 (Timestamp tab), EXP-171 (strawman audit), EXP-172 (dashboard), EXP-173 (AI tab), EXP-176 (Part 4.1). A future holistic reviewer may benefit from a single index — possibly a new entry in monitor/changes/ or a short roll-up in the Overview tab — tying the five changes to the five coverage vehicles and the five open issues. This is a process optimization, not a defect in our current prose.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: consider a single paragraph in the Overview tab (or in Section 8.17 if it grows further) summarizing the April 2026 reactive-update wave as a documentary/architectural cluster. Low priority.

stronger_arguments.description: If ISS-REACTIVE-WIN-COLLISION is filed and fixed, the strongest framing turns the disambiguation into evidence for the acknowledge-without-fix pattern: the author has now implemented three parallel acknowledgements without structural fixes — methodology.json (OTS scope scoped but prediction/observation files still not separated), registry_counts self-caveat (display ratio disavowed at API but still headlined on wins.html), and registry_integrity_notes disambiguation (API-level claim_keys but visible-prose WIN duplicates unchanged). Three acknowledgements, zero structural fixes, all shipped within a single 24-hour window. That pattern is stronger evidence of intent than any one acknowledgement in isolation. This reframes the April 2026 cluster as a coherent strategy rather than five disconnected updates.
sources: wins.html visible duplicate WIN-058 and WIN-062 rows (dome site — needs re-fetch for live verification)

stronger_arguments.description: A counterpoint consideration (per user-preference instruction to think about the other side): a dome defender can legitimately argue the API-layer disambiguation IS the fix, and the duplicate visible-prose labels are a lossy rendering choice rather than a scientific problem. That argument has weight if the API becomes the canonical form readers are directed to; as of April 2026, ai_manifest.json does direct AI agents to claim_index.json before wins.html. But human readers of wins.html do not follow that read-order, and the site headline count ('69 confirmed') is rendered against the visible collision-containing prose, not the disambiguated API. A fair version of our critique must therefore credit the API fix as genuine progress (documentary move, not merely rhetorical) while observing that the user-facing headline still relies on the pre-disambiguation data. That is the documentary-fixed-at-API, presentation-unfixed-at-HTML framing that aligns with ISS-1088.

cross_check_results.pending_expansions: EXP-122 is the only pending item and is cancelled — expansion pipeline is otherwise empty of work on the April 2026 change set. Twelve new EXPs (170-177 plus suffixed variants) landed between 2026-04-17 and 2026-04-18 addressing the reactive changes.
cross_check_results.pending_human_notes_analyst_side: HNOTE-DOMAIN-001 (consumed — drove EXP-172), HNOTE-AI-TAB-001 (consumed — drove EXP-173), HNOTE-PART4-DOMAINS-001 (no status — drove EXP-176). No analyst-side human notes remain pending on the April 2026 change set.
cross_check_results.open_issues_on_april_2026_changes: ISS-1074 (daily-ai-review monitor), ISS-1087, ISS-1088, ISS-1089 (all Timestamp Error tab refinements), ISS-1103, ISS-1105 (SEC-2b.5 refinements). All known, all assigned to the queue appropriately.
cross_check_results.closed_issues_relevant: ISS-1072 (closed via EXP-170 integration), ISS-1073 (closed via EXP-171 strawman audit), ISS-1076/1077 (WIN-032 stale-number patches), ISS-1084, ISS-1104 (SEC-8.17 patches).

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider/analyst pipeline has been remarkably thorough in responding to the April 17 reactive change wave. Five major dome-author changes in 24 hours generated seven EXPs plus two section integrations (2b.5 and 8.17), three closed issues (1072, 1073, and others), and six open refinement issues. The coverage ratio is high and the framing in the integrated prose (Section 8.17 and the Timestamp Error update block) is calibrated to credit documentary progress while maintaining the structural-fix critique. Only one WIN-prose gap was found, and it is small in scope (three WIN entries need a one-paragraph update each).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — no claim being refuted. This is a coverage audit, and the pipeline mostly passed.

deeper_analysis:
This meta-audit was triggered by CNOTE-REACTIVE-001 after the dome author shipped five substantive changes in a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17. The curmudgeon was asked to do the gap analysis Step 0c2 is designed for (major-change workload audit), but via the human-note path.

The five changes break down into two strategic clusters:

Cluster A (acknowledge-our-critique-then-deflect): chg-001 (methodology.json OTS scope concession), chg-002 (review-response.html strawman control), and parts of chg-005 (context.html relabeled archival, ai_manifest.json agent_rules telling AI auditors to read anti-strawman framing first). These are documentary/rhetorical moves that narrow the dome authors exposure without changing the underlying data or monitoring code.

Cluster B (acknowledge-data-integrity-without-fixing-it): chg-003 (daily-ai-review generating self-congratulatory summaries), chg-004 (registry_integrity_notes API-side disambiguation of WIN-058/062 while visible duplicates persist), parts of chg-005 (registry_semantics block disavowing the headline ratio at the API layer while keeping the ratio on the visible site). These are what ISS-1087 tracks for methodology.json; the same pattern is present in chg-004 and chg-005 but is only partially covered by existing work.

Our review pipelines response (EXP-170 through EXP-177) is well-calibrated for Cluster A: Section 8.17 catalogs the AI-perception moves and credits them as documentary, the Timestamp Error tab update block gives credit for methodology.json scoping while maintaining the structural-fix critique, and EXP-171 surgically neutralizes each of the five strawman labels. That cluster is done.

Cluster B is where the gap lives. The registry_integrity_notes disambiguation (chg-004) acknowledges a problem our review flagged, and the acknowledgement itself is a documentary concession worth citing in WIN-058, WIN-058b, and WIN-062 — ideally alongside the observation that the visible-prose collisions persist on wins.html even though the claim_index.json file now disambiguates them. This framing (documentary fix at API, presentation unfixed at HTML) is the same structure we use for methodology.json — a pattern the review can consolidate once a few more instances accumulate.

A dome-defender counterpoint is worth acknowledging: one could argue the API layer IS the canonical form, and visible duplicate WIN-058 rows are merely a rendering artifact. As of April 2026, ai_manifest.json explicitly directs AI agents to read claim_index.json before wins.html, which gives that argument some teeth for AI auditors (though not for human readers who still see the headline 69 confirmed rendered against pre-disambiguation prose). The strongest version of our response therefore credits the API fix as genuine documentary progress, notes the presentation layer has not been updated, and highlights that the headline count is still rendered against the pre-disambiguation visible prose. That framing is compatible with ISS-1088s documentary-vs-cryptographic split and would benefit from being unified across the Timestamp Error tab, Section 2b.5, and the new WIN-058/062 paragraphs.

A secondary finding: no single document in our review currently catalogs the April 2026 reactive-change set as a coherent cluster. The pieces are all present (Section 8.17, Timestamp Error update, Section 2b.5, Part 4.1 update, three open issues) but a future reader has to assemble them. A small summary paragraph in the Overview tab tying the cluster together would improve navigation without adding new content. Low priority.

Recommended next action: decider creates a new issue asking the analyst to append a short registry_integrity_notes paragraph to WIN-058, WIN-058b, and WIN-062 detail_evidence. One expansion, three WIN targets, cross-reference to ISS-1087 for the pattern framing. Everything else in the April 2026 change set is already handled or explicitly tracked.
